text:
Once upon a time, in a small village surrounded by woods, there lived a wise old owl named Oliver. Oliver loved his owl perch high in the oak tree, watching over his quiet world.

One rainy afternoon, Oliver heard soft whispers wind through the trees. Curious, he called out. Two mice giggled, sharing secrets.

"Why do we chatter?" asked the first.

"It's nice," replied the second. "Like giggles."

Oliver chuckled softly. "You don't know how glad I am for my chuckles."

Later, shadows stretched long. Oliver felt a tug on his branch. It was Maya, wise as he.

"Advice?" Oliver hooted.

"Just dream big," she chirped.

Days turned to nights. Oliver dreamed soaring. Sky felt endless.

When spring bloom'd, so did hope.

Oliver sang ancient songs. Flowers swayed to his tune.

We're heroes together.

Stars watched. Friendship’s fiery.

Moonlight's soft. We glow
